mappings.episodeTitle: The Zulu Nation's Rise and Fall
mappings.rationaleAI: The Zulu Nation's formation under Shaka (c. 1816), the mfecane, the Anglo-Zulu War of 1879 (Isandlwana, Ulundi), and the kingdom's destruction under British colonialism directly correspond to C.4 (rise of the Zulu Empire under Shaka) and C.7 (Transvaal-Pedi and Zulu wars).
The Berlin Conference (1884-85) and the Scramble for Africa provide the broader context for C.7 (era of mineral discoveries and confederation) at the section's terminal date of c. 1885.
The Dutch East India Company's trading posts from the Cape onward are directly relevant to C.3 on the Dutch settlement at the Cape of Good Hope from 1652 and expansion toward the Orange River.

The East India Company's role as an instrument of British imperial expansion provides context for C.6 on British colonial settlements and the Cape eastern frontier, including the British colonies of Natal and Cape Colony.
